title: Parkio Daily for June 12, 2026
teaser: Heat index hits 100+ this weekend in Orlando; new America 250th treats debut; Bullseye popcorn bucket arrives soon.

rides.name: TRON Lightcycle / Run
rides.note: Shortest waits in first operating hour

breaking.title: Dangerous heat index forecast for Orlando this weekend
breaking.body: Central Florida meteorologists predict heat index values reaching 100 degrees June 13–14. Intense summer heat is typical but these readings are on the higher end for mid-June.

parkioInsight.text: Indoor attractions and air-conditioned quick-service spots tend to be your best bet during peak afternoon hours. Take breaks every 90 minutes and hydrate before you feel thirsty.

bignews.title: New baby animals arrive at Disney's Animal Kingdom
bignews.body: Spring brought a wave of births behind the scenes at Animal Kingdom, with several new animals now visible to guests. The park celebrates the arrivals as part of ongoing conservation work.

parkioInsight.text: The Kilimanjaro Safaris route usually offers the best glimpses of newborns during early morning hours. Cast members near the loading area can often point out where families are resting.

bignews.title: Logo Mania merchandise collection now available at parks
bignews.body: A new colorful merchandise line celebrating Disney attractions, lands, and transportation options is rolling out. The collection features six designs highlighting favorite park icons.

parkioInsight.text: New collections like this tend to sell out at flagship shops by mid-afternoon on busy days. Stopping at Emporium or World of Disney before 11am usually improves selection.

bignews.title: America 250th anniversary treats debut across Walt Disney World
bignews.body: Disney chefs unveiled a red, white, and blue menu for the nation's 250th anniversary. Items include Fourth of July beignets and other patriotic snacks available at multiple locations.

parkioInsight.text: Festival-style food offerings often draw lines at outdoor kiosks by lunchtime. Grabbing a specialty item mid-morning or after 7pm usually saves 10–15 minutes of queue time.

bignews.title: Sam Eagle popcorn bucket coming to Walt Disney World
bignews.body: A patriotic Sam Eagle popcorn bucket will launch alongside the America 250th celebration menu. A new Hoop-Dee-Doo Musical Revue mug also debuts as part of the anniversary collection.

parkioInsight.text: Character-themed popcorn buckets tend to sell out within hours at major carts. Checking locations near park entrances right at rope drop often secures one before the rush.

topstories.title: Bullseye popcorn bucket released at Hong Kong, coming to US parks soon
topstories.body: Hong Kong Disneyland launched a rideable Bullseye popcorn bucket from Toy Story. The design is confirmed to arrive at both Disneyland Resort and Walt Disney World in the near future.

parkioInsight.text: New popcorn buckets often appear without advance notice at flagship stands. Checking with cast members at main popcorn locations each morning can help you spot the drop day.

topstories.title: Disney Springs bus rule may return for Fourth of July weekend
topstories.body: An unpopular transportation restriction at Disney Springs could come back next month for the July 4 holiday. The rule has been used during peak crowd periods in the past.

parkioInsight.text: Disney Springs bus service tends to slow significantly on major holidays. Arriving before 5pm or using rideshare during evening hours usually avoids the longest waits.

topstories.title: Sorcerer Mickey pullover hoodie now on sale at Hollywood Studios
topstories.body: A new denim-blue hoodie featuring the iconic Sorcerer Mickey hat design is available. The pullover retails for $69.99 and ties into the recent return of the hat symbol to the park.

parkioInsight.text: Apparel tied to park icons often moves quickly at main retail locations. Stopping at Mickey's of Hollywood during early entry or late evening usually offers better size availability.

topstories.title: Three new deals launched at Disney Springs for summer
topstories.body: Disney Springs Resort Area Hotels are offering up to 25 percent off as teacher appreciation through September 7. Additional discounts on dining and entertainment also started this week.

parkioInsight.text: Disney Springs dining deals tend to be valid Sunday through Thursday. Booking reservations mid-week usually improves availability and maximizes discount value.

topstories.title: Ghirardelli temporary relocation underway at Disney Springs
topstories.body: The Ghirardelli Soda Fountain and Chocolate Shop is moving from Marketplace to Town Center during construction. A recent permit reveals the permanent space will open in 2027.

parkioInsight.text: The temporary Town Center location may have reduced seating and longer waits during the transition. Visiting during off-peak afternoon hours tends to keep lines manageable.

topstories.title: Disneyland ordered to shut down gas-powered Autopia by 2027
topstories.body: California government records confirm Disneyland must close the gas-powered Autopia by 2027. The park announced nearly two years ago it would transition the opening-day attraction to electric engines.

parkioInsight.text: Autopia will likely close for an extended refurbishment to complete the engine swap. Riding the attraction during your next Disneyland trip may be your last chance before the conversion.

topstories.title: Wagyu now available at EPCOT quick-service location
topstories.body: A new wagyu dish launched at a popular EPCOT quick-service spot. The upscale offering joins the menu at a counter-service restaurant in the park.

parkioInsight.text: Quick-service locations with premium menu items tend to run out by mid-afternoon on busy days. Ordering before 1pm or after 8pm usually ensures availability.

icymi.title: Margaret Kerry, Tinker Bell model, passes away at 97
icymi.body: Margaret Kerry, who served as the live-action reference model for Tinker Bell in Peter Pan (1953), died June 11. Animator Marc Davis selected Kerry to pantomime for the iconic character.

icymi.title: Tomorrowland performer Michael Iceberg dies
icymi.body: Michael Iseberg, known as Michael Iceberg, passed away June 9 at Johns Hopkins Hospital. Iceberg performed in Tomorrowland and was remembered by fans and friends this week.

icymi.title: Flags at Walt Disney World lowered for Pulse Remembrance Day
icymi.body: American flags across Walt Disney World are at half-staff June 12 to honor the anniversary of the Pulse nightclub shooting. Governor Ron DeSantis ordered the statewide tribute.

spotlight.title: My Disney Experience app gets major update
spotlight.body: A significant change rolled out to the My Disney Experience app this week. The update affects how guests interact with park features and plan their day, though specific details are still emerging.

parkioInsight.text: App updates can occasionally cause temporary glitches in the first 48 hours. Checking your Lightning Lane selections and dining reservations early in the morning helps catch any sync issues before park arrival.
